what_it_does: Declares the REGISTER/theme axis as a module-level AXIS dict: id/namespace 'register', a single discrete field 'theme', value_source 'live', and a description framing it as the mood/finish (the DNA language/theme) the renderer resolves through.
observations: AXIS dict: id='register', namespace='register', fields={'theme':'discrete'}, value_source='live'
desc names a concrete theme example 'the dark observatory'
desc states the renderer resolves THROUGH this axis via archetype.language -> DNA's grammar/tokens/voice
desc claims re-resolving the register re-skins every surface (served-cascade onto color.primary)
standouts: Frames register as the finish the renderer resolves through, tying theme to a DNA language/archetype grammar
conventions: Follows an AXIS declaration pattern (id, namespace, fields, value_source, desc) parallel to axes/resolution.py
Pure declarative data module, no logic
notes: If briefing someone: this is the theme/mood axis declaration — choosing a register (e.g. dark-observatory) re-skins every served surface.
questions: Where is the set of available themes defined and how does value_source 'live' resolve them?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — module comment and AXIS dict both describe the register/theme/finish axis.
intention_signals: Tries to make visual mood/finish a single resolvable axis so re-resolving it re-skins all surfaces through the DNA grammar/tokens/voice.
novelty: Treating 'register'/theme as a renderer-resolved axis whose change cascades a full re-skin via archetype language.
contribution: Contributes the register/theme axis declaration — the mood/finish facet (theme, discrete, live) the renderer resolves through.
summary_for_embedding: Python AXIS declaration for the register/theme axis: id register, discrete 'theme' field, live value_source, framed as the mood/finish the renderer resolves through via DNA language to re-skin all surfaces.
intention_for_embedding: To define visual mood/finish (theme/DNA-language) as one live-resolved axis whose re-resolution re-skins every surface.
